'''成海神社'''（なるみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|緑区 (名古屋市)|Q1155209|緑区}}{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「成海神社」に比定される{{jalink|式内社}}。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 : {{jalink|日本武尊}} ; 配祀 : {{jalink|宮簀媛|Q11455216|宮簀媛命}} :: {{jalink|尾張国造|Q11465294}}の{{jalink|乎止与命|Q97706258}}と真敷刀俾命の子で、日本武尊の后の一人。{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}に赴く日本武尊から草薙剣を預かり、後に熱田神宮を創祀した。 : {{jalink|建稲種命|Q11486691}} :: 宮簀媛命の兄で、日本武尊の東征の際、副将軍として随従した。 === 歴史 === 『熱田太神宮御鎮座次第神体本紀』{{Efn|この書物は{{jalink|天応|Q3269361}}元年（{{jalink|781年|Q31175}}）の奥書をもつが、実際には江戸前期に成立した{{jalink|偽書|Q42757690}}とされる{{Sfn|三渡俊一郎|1974|p=94}}。}}によれば、{{jalink|朱鳥|Q777413}}元年（{{jalink|686年|Q30619}}）、{{jalink|草薙剣||草薙神剣}}が{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065|熱田}}に還座（{{jalink|草薙剣盗難事件|Q861812}}）された時に、{{jalink|日本武尊}}の縁由により創祀された10の神社のうちの一つという。創建当初は今より南の地、{{jalink|扇川|Q11496839}}に面した天神山に鎮座していたという。この天神山付近には鳴海廃寺があり、{{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}頃の複弁五葉半蓮華文軒丸瓦や軒平瓦が出土し、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の遺構と推定されている。現在、鳴海廃寺の跡地には竹林山圓龍寺が建っている。 平安中期成立の『延喜式神名帳』には愛智郡「成海神社」と記載があり、これが当社を指していることが確実視されている。「成海」は鳴海の古表記で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の『{{jalink|正倉院文書|Q11545362}}』や平安中期の『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に「愛智郡成海郷」の名がみえる。{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}について、『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本は「従三位上 成海天神」、{{jalink|国府宮}}本は「正二位上 成海天神」としている。 {{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}元年、{{jalink|足利氏|Q725370}}の安原宗範により根古屋城（{{jalink|鳴海城|Q11675368}}）が築かれ、当社は現在の乙子山に奉遷されたという。現在の城跡内（城跡公園の東）には境外社天神社が鎮座している。例祭では御旅所となる。安原宗範の侍女が寄進したという鏡が当社に残っているという。{{jalink|鳴海八幡宮|Q11675363}}は当社の別宮として扱われ、戦国期には{{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}らが当社と鳴海八幡宮に{{jalink|神田|Q10855080}}を寄進した記録がある。中世の栄えた時期には30余りの社家があったと伝わり、{{jalink|室町期}}の{{jalink|寛正|Q1143928}}年間の{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}や{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}年間の祈年祭祝詞など中世の遺物が複数が残っている。 当社は、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}から{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}にかけて'''東宮大明神'''などと称され、愛知郡鳴海村の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}となっていた。{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}の東に位置することから「東宮」の名がついたとされ、『{{jalink|尾張国地名考}}』によれば{{jalink|神鏡|Q11591009}}の古額に{{jalink|小野道風|Q1439775}}が書いたという「東宮日月少」の文字があるという。江戸前期成立の『{{jalink|寛文村々覚書|Q137766629}}』には「鳴海村...氏神東宮大明神壱社 当村祢宜 藤太夫持分」とある。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}元年（{{jalink|1716年|Q6892}}）に京都吉田家より{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の神階を受けている。 江戸期の時点では牧野家が代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めていた。牧野家の{{jalink|本姓|Q11520116}}は{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の尾張稲麿から続く系譜の記録が残っている{{Sfn|加藤国光|1995|p=211}}。鳴海八幡宮の神官であった久野家も当社の神官を兼任していたが、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に牧野家と久野家が口論し、祭礼が分裂。以降は、当社と鳴海八幡宮は祭りなどを分かれて行うようになったという。 旧址天神山の南には{{jalink|天白川 (愛知県)|Q11443111|天白川}}支流の扇川が流れており、往古は洲一帯に「鳴海潟」という浜があった。天神社には日本武尊が鳴海潟で詠まれたという御歌「奈留美良乎　美也礼皮止保志　比多加知尓　己乃由不志保尓　和多良牟加毛（鳴海浦を見やれば遠し火高地にこの夕潮に渡らへむかも）」を刻んだ石碑がある。その他、日本武尊は鳴海について4首の御歌を残されている。御歌にある「火高（{{jalink|大高町 (名古屋市)|Q11441971|大高}}）」とは尾張国造城館の所在地で、日本武尊の后である宮簀媛命が住んでいた。現在、同地には熱田神宮境外摂社{{jalink|氷上姉子神社|Q11549548}}がある。東征からの帰還の際には、日本武尊は鳴海潟から火高まで船で渡ったという。毎年{{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}}（近年は同日に近い日曜日に開催）の例祭では、この神話に因み、御神霊を旧址に渡御し、木片一片を御船として扇川に流す御船流神事を行う。 === 主な年中行事 === * 2月節分の日：節分祭 * 7月中旬：夏祭茅ノ輪神事 * 10月第2日曜：鳴海祭 * 10月第2日曜：例祭、御船流神事 === 境内外末社 === {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * 神明社 * 熊野日向社 * 宝田社 * 白山社 * 山神社 * 道祖祓島社 * 御井社 * 日割金刀比羅社 * 今宮社 * 氷上社・源大夫社合殿 * 八幡社 * 八劔社 * 住吉社 * 多賀社 * 愛宕社 * 風神社 * 北野社 * 子安社 * 境外社：天神社 * 東宮稲荷社 * だるま塚 }} === 文化財など === * 鳴海祭（裏方）の山車行事と丹下・北浦・花井・城之下の山車　名古屋市無形民俗文化財 * {{jalink|弘治 (日本)|Q1143910|弘治}}3年（{{jalink|1557年|Q6595}}） - {{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}社領安堵状。 など === ギャラリー === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|鳥居 File:Narumijinja2.JPG|境内 File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|鳴海祭の山車曳き回し File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|山車のからくり人形 === 交通 === * {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}} {{jalink|鳴海駅|Q6966454}}より徒歩で約15分。 * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}{{jalink|名鉄バス知立営業所#鳴海線|Q11415723|鳴海線}}「成海神社」バス停より徒歩で約1分。 * {{jalink|名古屋市営バス|Q11414982}}{{jalink|名古屋市営バス緑営業所#新瑞12号系統|Q11414992|新瑞12}}・{{jalink|名古屋市営バス鳴尾営業所#鳴子15号系統|Q11414994|鳴子15号系統}}「花井」バス停より徒歩で約5分。 === 参考文献 === * {{jalink|尾張名所図会|Q11465290|『尾張名所図会』 第五巻　成海神社}}、{{jalink|1844年|Q7633}} === 脚注 === === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 外部リンク === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ 成海神社] - 公式サイト ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} 平安中期成立の『延喜式神名帳』には愛智郡「成海神社」と記載があり、これが当社を指していることが確実視されている。「成海」は鳴海の古表記で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の『{{jalink|正倉院文書|Q11545362}}』や平安中期の『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に「愛智郡成海郷」の名がみえる。{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}について、『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本は「従三位上 成海天神」、{{jalink|国府宮}}本は「正二位上 成海天神」としている。 {{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}元年、{{jalink|足利氏|Q725370}}の安原宗範により根古屋城（{{jalink|鳴海城|Q11675368}}）が築かれ、当社は現在の乙子山に奉遷されたという。現在の城跡内（城跡公園の東）には境外社天神社が鎮座している。例祭では御旅所となる。安原宗範の侍女が寄進したという鏡が当社に残っているという。{{jalink|鳴海八幡宮|Q11675363}}は当社の別宮として扱われ、戦国期には{{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}らが当社と鳴海八幡宮に{{jalink|神田|Q10855080}}を寄進した記録がある。中世の栄えた時期には30余りの社家があったと伝わり、{{jalink|室町期}}の{{jalink|寛正|Q1143928}}年間の{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}や{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}年間の祈年祭祝詞など中世の遺物が複数が残っている。 当社は、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}から{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}にかけて'''東宮大明神'''などと称され、愛知郡鳴海村の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}となっていた。{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}の東に位置することから「東宮」の名がついたとされ、『{{jalink|尾張国地名考}}』によれば{{jalink|神鏡|Q11591009}}の古額に{{jalink|小野道風|Q1439775}}が書いたという「東宮日月少」の文字があるという。江戸前期成立の『{{jalink|寛文村々覚書|Q137766629}}』には「鳴海村...氏神東宮大明神壱社 当村祢宜 藤太夫持分」とある。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}元年（{{jalink|1716年|Q6892}}）に京都吉田家より{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の神階を受けている。 江戸期の時点では牧野家が代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めていた。牧野家の{{jalink|本姓|Q11520116}}は{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の尾張稲麿から続く系譜の記録が残っている{{Sfn|加藤国光|1995|p=211}}。鳴海八幡宮の神官であった久野家も当社の神官を兼任していたが、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に牧野家と久野家が口論し、祭礼が分裂。以降は、当社と鳴海八幡宮は祭りなどを分かれて行うようになったという。 旧址天神山の南には{{jalink|天白川 (愛知県)|Q11443111|天白川}}支流の扇川が流れており、往古は洲一帯に「鳴海潟」という浜があった。天神社には日本武尊が鳴海潟で詠まれたという御歌「奈留美良乎　美也礼皮止保志　比多加知尓　己乃由不志保尓　和多良牟加毛（鳴海浦を見やれば遠し火高地にこの夕潮に渡らへむかも）」を刻んだ石碑がある。その他、日本武尊は鳴海について4首の御歌を残されている。御歌にある「火高（{{jalink|大高町 (名古屋市)|Q11441971|大高}}）」とは尾張国造城館の所在地で、日本武尊の后である宮簀媛命が住んでいた。現在、同地には熱田神宮境外摂社{{jalink|氷上姉子神社|Q11549548}}がある。東征からの帰還の際には、日本武尊は鳴海潟から火高まで船で渡ったという。毎年{{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}}（近年は同日に近い日曜日に開催）の例祭では、この神話に因み、御神霊を旧址に渡御し、木片一片を御船として扇川に流す御船流神事を行う。 === 主な年中行事 === * 2月節分の日：節分祭 * 7月中旬：夏祭茅ノ輪神事 * 10月第2日曜：鳴海祭 * 10月第2日曜：例祭、御船流神事 === 境内外末社 === {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * 神明社 * 熊野日向社 * 宝田社 * 白山社 * 山神社 * 道祖祓島社 * 御井社 * 日割金刀比羅社 * 今宮社 * 氷上社・源大夫社合殿 * 八幡社 * 八劔社 * 住吉社 * 多賀社 * 愛宕社 * 風神社 * 北野社 * 子安社 * 境外社：天神社 * 東宮稲荷社 * だるま塚 }} === 文化財など === * 鳴海祭（裏方）の山車行事と丹下・北浦・花井・城之下の山車　名古屋市無形民俗文化財 * {{jalink|弘治 (日本)|Q1143910|弘治}}3年（{{jalink|1557年|Q6595}}） - {{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}社領安堵状。 など === ギャラリー === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|鳥居 File:Narumijinja2.JPG|境内 File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|鳴海祭の山車曳き回し '''成海神社'''（なるみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|緑区 (名古屋市)|Q1155209|緑区}}{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「成海神社」に比定される{{jalink|式内社}}。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 : {{jalink|日本武尊}} ; 配祀 : {{jalink|宮簀媛|Q11455216|宮簀媛命}} :: {{jalink|尾張国造|Q11465294}}の{{jalink|乎止与命|Q97706258}}と真敷刀俾命の子で、日本武尊の后の一人。{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}に赴く日本武尊から草薙剣を預かり、後に熱田神宮を創祀した。 : {{jalink|建稲種命|Q11486691}} :: 宮簀媛命の兄で、日本武尊の東征の際、副将軍として随従した。 === 歴史 === 『熱田太神宮御鎮座次第神体本紀』{{Efn|この書物は{{jalink|天応|Q3269361}}元年（{{jalink|781年|Q31175}}）の奥書をもつが、実際には江戸前期に成立した{{jalink|偽書|Q42757690}}とされる{{Sfn|三渡俊一郎|1974|p=94}}。}}によれば、{{jalink|朱鳥|Q777413}}元年（{{jalink|686年|Q30619}}）、{{jalink|草薙剣||草薙神剣}}が{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065|熱田}}に還座（{{jalink|草薙剣盗難事件|Q861812}}）された時に、{{jalink|日本武尊}}の縁由により創祀された10の神社のうちの一つという。創建当初は今より南の地、{{jalink|扇川|Q11496839}}に面した天神山に鎮座していたという。この天神山付近には鳴海廃寺があり、{{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}頃の複弁五葉半蓮華文軒丸瓦や軒平瓦が出土し、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の遺構と推定されている。現在、鳴海廃寺の跡地には竹林山圓龍寺が建っている。 平安中期成立の『延喜式神名帳』には愛智郡「成海神社」と記載があり、これが当社を指していることが確実視されている。「成海」は鳴海の古表記で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の『{{jalink|正倉院文書|Q11545362}}』や平安中期の『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に「愛智郡成海郷」の名がみえる。{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}について、『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本は「従三位上 成海天神」、{{jalink|国府宮}}本は「正二位上 成海天神」としている。 旧址天神山の南には{{jalink|天白川 (愛知県)|Q11443111|天白川}}支流の扇川が流れており、往古は洲一帯に「鳴海潟」という浜があった。天神社には日本武尊が鳴海潟で詠まれたという御歌「奈留美良乎　美也礼皮止保志　比多加知尓　己乃由不志保尓　和多良牟加毛（鳴海浦を見やれば遠し火高地にこの夕潮に渡らへむかも）」を刻んだ石碑がある。その他、日本武尊は鳴海について4首の御歌を残されている。御歌にある「火高（{{jalink|大高町 (名古屋市)|Q11441971|大高}}）」とは尾張国造城館の所在地で、日本武尊の后である宮簀媛命が住んでいた。現在、同地には熱田神宮境外摂社{{jalink|氷上姉子神社|Q11549548}}がある。東征からの帰還の際には、日本武尊は鳴海潟から火高まで船で渡ったという。毎年{{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}}（近年は同日に近い日曜日に開催）の例祭では、この神話に因み、御神霊を旧址に渡御し、木片一片を御船として扇川に流す御船流神事を行う。 {{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = （伝）{{jalink|朱鳥|Q777413}}元年（{{jalink|686年|Q30619}}） | 本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} | 別名 = 東宮大明神

{{Location map|Nagoya|label=成海神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''成海神社'''（なるみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|名古屋市|Q11751}}{{jalink|緑区 (名古屋市)|Q1155209|緑区}}{{jalink|鳴海町 (名古屋市)|Q136913130|鳴海町}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|愛知郡 (愛知県)|Q552687|愛智郡}}「成海神社」に比定される{{jalink|式内社}}。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 : {{jalink|日本武尊}} ; 配祀 : {{jalink|宮簀媛|Q11455216|宮簀媛命}} :: {{jalink|尾張国造|Q11465294}}の{{jalink|乎止与命|Q97706258}}と真敷刀俾命の子で、日本武尊の后の一人。{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}に赴く日本武尊から草薙剣を預かり、後に熱田神宮を創祀した。 : {{jalink|建稲種命|Q11486691}} :: 宮簀媛命の兄で、日本武尊の東征の際、副将軍として随従した。 === 歴史 === 『熱田太神宮御鎮座次第神体本紀』{{Efn|この書物は{{jalink|天応|Q3269361}}元年（{{jalink|781年|Q31175}}）の奥書をもつが、実際には江戸前期に成立した{{jalink|偽書|Q42757690}}とされる{{Sfn|三渡俊一郎|1974|p=94}}。}}によれば、{{jalink|朱鳥|Q777413}}元年（{{jalink|686年|Q30619}}）、{{jalink|草薙剣||草薙神剣}}が{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065|熱田}}に還座（{{jalink|草薙剣盗難事件|Q861812}}）された時に、{{jalink|日本武尊}}の縁由により創祀された10の神社のうちの一つという。創建当初は今より南の地、{{jalink|扇川|Q11496839}}に面した天神山に鎮座していたという。この天神山付近には鳴海廃寺があり、{{jalink|8世紀|Q8086}}頃の複弁五葉半蓮華文軒丸瓦や軒平瓦が出土し、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}の遺構と推定されている。現在、鳴海廃寺の跡地には竹林山圓龍寺が建っている。 平安中期成立の『延喜式神名帳』には愛智郡「成海神社」と記載があり、これが当社を指していることが確実視されている。「成海」は鳴海の古表記で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の『{{jalink|正倉院文書|Q11545362}}』や平安中期の『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に「愛智郡成海郷」の名がみえる。{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}について、『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本は「従三位上 成海天神」、{{jalink|国府宮}}本は「正二位上 成海天神」としている。 {{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}元年、{{jalink|足利氏|Q725370}}の安原宗範により根古屋城（{{jalink|鳴海城|Q11675368}}）が築かれ、当社は現在の乙子山に奉遷されたという。現在の城跡内（城跡公園の東）には境外社天神社が鎮座している。例祭では御旅所となる。安原宗範の侍女が寄進したという鏡が当社に残っているという。{{jalink|鳴海八幡宮|Q11675363}}は当社の別宮として扱われ、戦国期には{{jalink|今川義元|Q1054305}}らが当社と鳴海八幡宮に{{jalink|神田|Q10855080}}を寄進した記録がある。中世の栄えた時期には30余りの社家があったと伝わり、{{jalink|室町期}}の{{jalink|寛正|Q1143928}}年間の{{jalink|棟札|Q8008834}}や{{jalink|応永|Q1193078}}年間の祈年祭祝詞など中世の遺物が複数が残っている。 当社は、{{jalink|中世|Q12554}}から{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}にかけて'''東宮大明神'''などと称され、愛知郡鳴海村の{{jalink|氏神|Q767655}}となっていた。{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}の東に位置することから「東宮」の名がついたとされ、『{{jalink|尾張国地名考}}』によれば{{jalink|神鏡|Q11591009}}の古額に{{jalink|小野道風|Q1439775}}が書いたという「東宮日月少」の文字があるという。江戸前期成立の『{{jalink|寛文村々覚書|Q137766629}}』には「鳴海村...氏神東宮大明神壱社 当村祢宜 藤太夫持分」とある。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}元年（{{jalink|1716年|Q6892}}）に京都吉田家より{{jalink|正一位|Q11123258}}の神階を受けている。 江戸期の時点では牧野家が代々{{jalink|神官|Q11589454}}を務めていた。牧野家の{{jalink|本姓|Q11520116}}は{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}で、{{jalink|奈良時代|Q189178}}の尾張稲麿から続く系譜の記録が残っている{{Sfn|加藤国光|1995|p=211}}。鳴海八幡宮の神官であった久野家も当社の神官を兼任していたが、{{jalink|元禄|Q1325001}}13年（{{jalink|1700年|Q6813}}）に牧野家と久野家が口論し、祭礼が分裂。以降は、当社と鳴海八幡宮は祭りなどを分かれて行うようになったという。 旧址天神山の南には{{jalink|天白川 (愛知県)|Q11443111|天白川}}支流の扇川が流れており、往古は洲一帯に「鳴海潟」という浜があった。天神社には日本武尊が鳴海潟で詠まれたという御歌「奈留美良乎　美也礼皮止保志　比多加知尓　己乃由不志保尓　和多良牟加毛（鳴海浦を見やれば遠し火高地にこの夕潮に渡らへむかも）」を刻んだ石碑がある。その他、日本武尊は鳴海について4首の御歌を残されている。御歌にある「火高（{{jalink|大高町 (名古屋市)|Q11441971|大高}}）」とは尾張国造城館の所在地で、日本武尊の后である宮簀媛命が住んでいた。現在、同地には熱田神宮境外摂社{{jalink|氷上姉子神社|Q11549548}}がある。東征からの帰還の際には、日本武尊は鳴海潟から火高まで船で渡ったという。毎年{{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}}（近年は同日に近い日曜日に開催）の例祭では、この神話に因み、御神霊を旧址に渡御し、木片一片を御船として扇川に流す御船流神事を行う。 * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市緑区鳴海町字乙子山85', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Narumi Jinja}} [[JA:成海神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11495515}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Narumi Shrine | image = [[File:Narumi-Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209}}{{jalink|Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} ; Co-enshrined * {{jalink|Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{jalink|Kusanagi sword}} to {{jalink|Atsuta Shrine|Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{jalink|Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '愛知県名古屋市緑区鳴海町字乙子山85', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Narumi Jinja}} [[JA:成海神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11495515}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Narumi Shrine | image = [[File:Narumi-Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209}}{{jalink|Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} ; Co-enshrined * {{jalink|Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{jalink|Kusanagi sword}} to {{jalink|Atsuta Shrine|Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{jalink|Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]] [[Category:need translation]]

* {'text': '愛知県名古屋市緑区鳴海町字乙子山85', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Narumi Jinja}} [[JA:成海神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11495515}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Narumi Shrine | image = Haiden | location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209}}{{jalink|Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} ; Co-enshrined * {{jalink|Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{jalink|Kusanagi sword}} to {{jalink|Atsuta Shrine|Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{jalink|Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden | location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}{{jalink|Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209}}{{jalink|Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}; former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{jalink|Nagoya|Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru}} ; Co-enshrined * {{jalink|Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{jalink|Kusanagi sword}} to {{jalink|Atsuta Shrine|Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{jalink|Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{jalink|Narumi Station|Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - [[File:Narumi-Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill||lt=Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill||lt=Yamato Takeru|UNKNOWN}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill||lt=Kusanagi sword|UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なるみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

[[File:Narumi-Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:名古屋市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の建築物]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]][[Category:qq]]

Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]]

[[File:Narumi-Jinja Haiden, Midori Ward Nagoya 2015.JPG|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Midori-ku, Nagoya|Q1155209|WD=Q1155209|1=Midori-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Narumi-cho (Nagoya)|Q136913130|WD=Q136913130|1=Narumi-chō, Nagoya}} Otoyama 85 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|5|12|N|136|57|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}; former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) 686 | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | other_name = Togu Daimyojin, Togu Myojin, Togu, Narumi Tenjin | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | rituals = Ofune-nagashi ritual }} '''Narumi Shrine''' (なるみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Narumi-cho, Midori Ward, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|WD=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, Aichi Prefecture. It is identified with the "Narumi Shrine" listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for Aichi District, Owari Province. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} ; Co-enshrined * {{ill|lt=Miyazu-hime|Q11455216|WD=Q11455216|lt=Miyazu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Takenainadane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|WD=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === A late source says Narumi Shrine was one of ten shrines established in connection with the return of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kusanagi sword|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Shrine|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} in 686. It was first at Tenjinyama near the Ougigawa River, where remains of Narumi temple ruins have been linked to a former shrine-temple complex. The ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' lists "Narumi Shrine" in Aichi District, and this is widely taken to refer to this shrine. In medieval times, Narumi Castle was built and the shrine is said to have been moved to present Otoyama. Narumi Hachimangu was treated as a separate shrine related to Narumi Shrine. From medieval to early modern periods it was also called "Togu Daimyojin" and served as the ujigami of Narumi village. It received the rank of Shoichii from the Yoshida house in 1716. A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] - official site ** {{Instagram|narumijinja.official}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Narumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:名古屋市緑区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Midori-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:鳴海町]]

A local tradition records that Yamato Takeru composed poems at Narumi Inlet and crossed by boat toward Odaka. The annual autumn festival includes an "Ofune-nagashi" rite in which a small wooden boat is floated down the river in memory of this legend. === Annual events === * Setsubun festival (around February) * Summer chinowa rite (mid-July) * Narumi festival (second Sunday of October) * Grand festival and Ofune-nagashi ritual (around October 10) === Subsidiary shrines === Includes numerous precinct and outside shrines such as Shinmei, Kumano-Hyuga, Hakusan, Hachiman, Sumiyoshi, Atago, Kitano, Koyasu, Tenjin (outside precinct), Togu Inari, and others. === Cultural properties === * Narumi Festival float events (Urahata side) and floats of Tange, Kitaura, Hanai, and Jonoshita - Nagoya City designated intangible folk cultural property * 1557 document by {{ill|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|Q1054305|WD=Q1054305|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} confirming shrine lands === Gallery === {{commonscat-inline|Narumi Jinja}} File:Narumijinja1.JPG|Torii File:Narumijinja2.JPG|Precinct File:Narumijinja3.JPG File:Narumimatsuri1.JPG|Narumi Festival float procession File:Narumimatsuri2.JPG File:Narumimatsuri3.JPG|Karakuri doll on festival float === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line {{ill|lt=Narumi Station|Q6966454|WD=Q6966454|1=Narumi Station}} * About 1 minute on foot from Meitetsu Bus "Narumi Jinja" stop * About 5 minutes on foot from Nagoya City Bus "Hanai" stop === References === * ''Owari Meisho Zue'' Vol. 5, Narumi Shrine (1844) === Notes === === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://narumi-jinja.or.jp/ Narumi Shrine] -
